title: The Compiled Core Chen 2026
content:
# The Compiled Core Chen 2026

Creator: Bo Chen
Date: 2026-02-24T00:00:00Z
Downloads: 0
Subjects: organ procurement, OPO, CMS-3409-P, regulatory compliance, artificial intelligence

The capstone paper of the AORTA (Autonomous OPO Reasoning and Triage Architecture) research program, synthesizing four prior publications into a unified framework for regulatory AI alignment in organ procurement. This paper identifies the deepest structural insight of the research program: that the reasoning methodology developed across the series is not input to a language model, but source code for a deterministic system that can be compiled and executed without AI for the vast majority of operational queries. The architecture treats large language models as compilers—offline tools that manufacture reasoning artifacts—rather than as runtimes invoked on every query. A three-tier system dispatches queries to fully compiled deterministic lookup (70–80%), grammar-guided constrained generation (15–25%), or full compositional reasoning (5–10%). The compiled tier requires no LLM, no API, no GPU, and no internet connection; it runs on commodity hardware in microseconds with zero hallucinations. The framework includes a compositional grammar of eight typed reasoning primitives, a validated trace dataset organized in four layers, a safety automaton that enforces the Human Line by topology, and a nine-field audit-grade output contract. All specifications are released under open license for adoption by all 56 U.S. organ procurement organizations.
